sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ariel Global Fund Added AT&T (T) as Fiber Expansion and Wireless Convergence Drive Long-Term Growth
feed.summary: Ariel Global Fund, managed by Ariel Investments, added AT&T Inc. (T) to its portfolio, citing the telecommunications company's fiber network expansion and wireless convergence as key drivers for long-term growth. The fund delivered strong results in 2025, outperforming benchmarks despite lagging in Q4. AT&T closed at approximately $28.78 per share on March 30, 2026, with a market capitalization of about $200.71 billion.

feed.title: Tulare student earns $50K scholarship
feed.summary: Tulare Western High School student Lauren Starr was awarded a $50,000 scholarship from Southern California Edison to pursue a college education in STEM. Starr plans to attend Loyola Marymount University to study math and join the women's water polo team, with aspirations of becoming a math teacher. The Edison Scholars program, which has awarded over $22 million in scholarships since 2006, recognizes high school seniors in SCE's service area who demonstrate academic achievement, financial need, and a passion for STEM.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) Shareholders
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP is alerting PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) shareholders who suffered losses between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026, about an upcoming deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal made materially false statements regarding its business execution, particularly concerning Branded Checkout initiatives, leading to a significant stock price drop following a surprise leadership change and a disappointing Q4 2025 earnings report. Investors are urged to contact the firm by April 20, 2026, to learn more about pursuing claims.

feed.title: Rail News - CSX 'fell short' of expectations in 2025, Angel says. For Railroad Career Professionals
feed.summary: CSX's 2025 financial results "fell short" of expectations, with revenue down 3% to $14.1 billion, an operating margin of 32.1%, and earnings per diluted share of $1.54. Despite the decline driven by lower export coal revenue and reduced merchandise volumes, President and CEO Steve Angel stated that work completed in 2025 positions the Class I railroad for stronger performance in 2026. Key achievements in 2025 included the reopening of the Howard Street Tunnel, significant infrastructure rebuilds, and improvements in safety metrics.

feed.title: Apollo Global Management, Inc. (APO) Shareholders Who Lost Money Have Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP has announced a securities fraud class action lawsuit against Apollo Global Management, Inc. (APO) on behalf of investors who suffered losses. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo's leadership, including CEO Marc Rowan and former CEO Leon Black, had undisclosed communications with Jeffrey Epstein regarding company business, rendering Apollo's claims of no business dealings with Epstein untrue and damaging the company's reputation. Investors who purchased shares between May 10, 2021, and February 21, 2026, have until May 1, 2026, to seek to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Snowflake Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against Snowflake Inc. (NYSE: SNOW), alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased Snowflake securities between June 27, 2023, and February 28, 2024. The firm accuses Snowflake of misleading investors by failing to disclose that product efficiency gains and tiered storage pricing would negatively impact consumption and revenues, and by denying the impending resignation of Defendant Slootman.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Gartner, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. and some of its officers, alleging violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims that Gartner made false and misleading statements regarding its contract value growth, projected revenues, and consulting segment performance from February 4, 2025, to February 2, 2026. Investors who purchased Gartner securities during this period are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of May 18, 2026.

feed.title: [8-K] HERSHEY CO Reports Material Event
feed.summary: The Hershey Company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 financial outlook, projecting net sales growth of 4% to 5% and adjusted earnings per share growth of 30% to 35%. This announcement was made during its Investor Day where the company also outlined its strategy to "Lead Next Generation Snacking," focusing on portfolio expansion, an integrated commercial model, and supply chain modernization. The reiterated guidance signals confidence in its long-term strategy for balanced growth and shareholder returns.

feed.title: Kroger Closes Two Houston Stores in Heights and Spring
feed.summary: Kroger has closed two Houston-area stores, one in the Heights and another in Spring, as part of a broader company-wide efficiency initiative. These closures are part of Kroger's plan to shut down about 60 stores nationally over an 18-month period, impacting local shoppers and employees. While affected staff are offered transfers, the immediate future of these retail spaces and the long-term impact on local grocery options remain subjects of discussion.

feed.title: Insurance Update: Wawanesa acquiring Everest Insurance Co. of Canada
feed.summary: Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company, a Winnipeg-based insurer, is acquiring Everest Insurance Company of Canada, the Canadian retail arm of Everest Group, Ltd. This acquisition will expand Wawanesa's commercial capabilities by adding approximately $305 million in annual commercial premiums, focusing on specialty commercial lines like cyber, aviation, and fleet insurance. Everest will retain legacy liabilities, and the deal is expected to close in Q4 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: Oracle Begins Employee Layoffs to Reduce Costs During AI Infrastructure Expansion
feed.summary: Oracle has initiated global layoffs impacting thousands of employees, including software engineers in its cloud division, to manage costs associated with its aggressive AI infrastructure expansion. The company has taken on over $58 billion in new debt for data center construction, pushing its total debt above $100 billion. These strategic job cuts aim to free up significant cash flow, with estimated savings of $8 billion to $10 billion, to sustain massive AI investments despite projected negative free cash flow until around 2030.

feed.title: GM Temporarily Idles 1,300 Workers at Factory Zero Amid Weakening EV Demand
feed.summary: General Motors has temporarily laid off 1,300 workers at its Factory Zero plant in Detroit due to weakening electric vehicle demand and recent US policy changes. This action is part of a broader restructuring of GM's EV strategy, which has already incurred $7.6 billion in impairments and thousands of job cuts. The affected employees are expected to return on April 13, with GM confirming that it is adjusting production to align with market demand.

feed.title: KLA (KLAC) Announces an Additional $7 Billion Share Buyback and Dividend Increase
feed.summary: KLA Corporation (NASDAQ:KLAC) announced a new $7 billion share buyback program and a 21% increase in its quarterly dividend to $2.30 per share. This adds to its existing buyback authorization and comes as the company benefits from high demand for semiconductor production tools, driven by AI processors and memory chips. KLA has seen significant stock appreciation, with shares up over 20% this year following a 93% rise last year, supported by continuous spending on data centers and chip manufacturing.

feed.title: Henry Schein Inc Stock: A Steady Force in Dental and Medical Distribution for North American Investo
feed.summary: Henry Schein Inc is a leading distributor of dental, medical, and veterinary products, holding a robust position in North American healthcare supply chains. The company's diversified operations, strong distribution network, and strategic investments in digital dentistry and technology-enabled services contribute to its resilience. For investors, Henry Schein offers exposure to steady demand in healthcare essentials, supported by a dividend policy and lower volatility compared to pure tech or pharma peers.

feed.title: Class I Briefs: CSX, BNSF, UP
feed.summary: CSX has opened its new TRANSFLO Petersburg Terminal in Virginia, expanding its transloading capabilities. BNSF received the Building Together Award from the Economic Development Corporation of Utah for its significant role in the state's economic prosperity and connectivity. Union Pacific's engineering team created a highly accurate 3D digital twin of the Big Boy No. 4014 steam locomotive to aid in planning its upcoming coast-to-coast tour for America's 250th birthday celebration.

feed.title: Japan Post unit trims AFLAC (NYSE: AFL) stake, retains 51.9M shares
feed.summary: Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd., a ten percent owner of Aflac Inc. (NYSE: AFL), reported indirect open-market sales of 14,500 shares of Aflac common stock on March 27, 2026. These sales were executed in three blocks at weighted average prices between $106.60 and $108.45 per share. After these transactions, the J&A Alliance Trust, associated with Japan Post, continues to hold 51,989,200 shares of Aflac common stock.

feed.title: Teradata Corp Stock: Constellation ShortList Recognition Highlights Strength in Hybrid Multicloud Analytics Platforms
feed.summary: Teradata Corp has been recognized on the 2026 Constellation ShortList for Hybrid and Multicloud Analytical Data Platforms, highlighting its strong capabilities in AI-driven enterprise solutions. This signals Teradata's competitive edge in meeting the growing demand for flexible data architectures and positions it favorably for investors in the analytics and cloud sectors. The company's focus on hybrid and multicloud solutions, robust governance, and integration of AI in its Vantage platform are key differentiators in a competitive market.

feed.title: Devon Energy Stock: Strong Momentum and Strategic Positioning in North American Oil and Gas
feed.summary: Devon Energy (ISIN: US25179M1036) shows robust momentum with shares significantly up, driven by positive analyst revisions. The company operates in key U.S. basins, focusing on free cash flow generation and returning capital to shareholders through variable dividends and share repurchases. Devon’s strategic positioning, strong financial discipline, and leverage to commodity prices make it an attractive investment for North American investors.

feed.title: West Expands Dublin Facility to Support High-Volume Injectable Therapies, Including Diabetes and Obesity
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services has opened a new 165,000 square foot facility in Dublin, significantly expanding its contract services to meet the growing global demand for high-volume injectable treatments, particularly for diabetes and obesity. This expansion enhances West's capabilities in drug handling, device assembly, and packaging, reinforcing its role in accelerating time to market for life-enhancing therapies. The new facility forms part of West Vantage™, the company's comprehensive contract services business, offering a single-source solution from product conceptualization to regulatory support.

feed.title: Emerson Electric, Crane, Toll Brothers, Mobileye, and GE Vernova Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Emerson Electric, Crane, Toll Brothers, Mobileye, and GE Vernova, saw their prices increase following news that President Trump was considering de-escalation in the military conflict in Iran, which helped stabilize oil prices. This positive sentiment led to a rebound in major indexes, with the S&P 500 jumping over 1%. The article highlights specific gains for each company and details GE Vernova's performance, noting its volatility and significant gains since the beginning of the year and its IPO.

feed.title: Packaging Corp (NYSE: PKG) Exec VP gains shares as 200% TSR units vest
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America's Executive VP, Donald R. Shirley, received 6,254 shares of common stock from TSR performance units and 478 shares from dividend equivalents, which vested at 200% of the performance measure on March 27, 2026. The company withheld 2,650 shares at $212.25 each to cover tax obligations. Following these transactions, Shirley directly holds 31,110 shares and indirectly holds 6,482 shares through a 401(k) plan.

feed.title: BIIB: Acquisition accelerates growth in immunology and nephrology, boosting long-term revenue and EPS
feed.summary: Biogen Inc. (BIIB) has announced a $5.6 billion acquisition that will add two best-in-class products in immunology and rare diseases, accelerating its expansion into nephrology. This strategic move is expected to be accretive by 2027 and has strong long-term growth prospects, leveraging combined commercial capabilities to boost revenue and EPS.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share at $494
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant shareholder of Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), recently acquired 1 share of the company's common stock for $494.25. This transaction, occurring on March 30, 2026, increases Horizon Kinetics' direct ownership to 3,467,936 shares. The purchase follows TPL's strong stock performance, surging over 50% in the last six months, and recent news including KeyBanc raising its price target on the stock due to developments in power generation and data center opportunities.

feed.title: Jefferies Downgrades Rockwell Automation (ROK) with Lowered Price Target | ROK Stock News
feed.summary: Jefferies has downgraded Rockwell Automation (ROK) from "Buy" to "Hold" and significantly lowered its price target by 22.45% to $380.00 from $490.00. This adjustment reflects changing market conditions and assessments for the industrial automation company. Despite the downgrade, other analysts have varying ratings and price targets, with the average target suggesting an upside of 19.04% from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: RTX subsidiary wins $966.7M MDA contract
feed.summary: Raytheon, a subsidiary of RTX, has been awarded two contract modifications totaling $966.7 million by the Missile Defense Agency to support the AN/TPY-2 radar system. These modifications will fund research, development, and sustainment of the radar, which is crucial for ballistic missile defense. The contracts extend the work through October 2030, significantly increasing the radar development contract's total ceiling value.

feed.title: Cummins Inc Stock: Engineered for Power and Reliability in Industrial Markets
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. (US2310211063) is highlighted for its strong fundamentals, dividend stability, and exposure to key industrial sectors like engines and electrification. The company maintains a leading position in diesel engines and power generation, while strategically investing in zero-emissions technologies through its Accelera segment. North American investors gain exposure to resilient sectors and energy transition plays, though risks include cyclical demand and evolving regulations.

feed.title: Can Dynamics 365 Growth Unlock Further Upside for Microsoft Stock?
feed.summary: Microsoft's Dynamics 365 business applications platform is becoming a significant contributor to the company's revenue growth, driven by sustained enterprise demand and AI integration. The platform saw a 19% revenue increase in Q2 fiscal 2026, supported by AI-powered agents and expanding partnerships. While competitive intensity from Oracle and Salesforce exists, Microsoft differentiates through tighter integration, positioning Dynamics 365 as a unified enterprise platform, though Microsoft's shares have underperformed the general sector over the past six months.

feed.title: IT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Gartner, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is reminding investors of a class action lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) for alleged securities fraud. Investors who purchased Gartner securities between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026, are encouraged to contact the firm before May 18, 2026, to potentially lead the lawsuit. The complaint alleges that Gartner made false and misleading statements regarding its ability to minimize seasonality risks, the growth potential of its contract value, and the improvement of business with "tariff impacted companies," which allegedly led to investor damages when the truth was revealed.

feed.title: Organon & Co stock hits 52-week low at $5.76
feed.summary: Organon & Co (OGN) stock recently hit a 52-week low of $5.76, reflecting a significant 61.11% decline over the past year. Despite this downturn, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is in oversold territory based on RSI and is undervalued compared to its Fair Value. The company has recently presented data for its VTAMA cream, licensed MIUDELLA, and completed an independent review that found no misconduct.

feed.title: Sallie Mae and Nubank Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Sallie Mae (NASDAQ:SLM) and Nubank (NYSE:NU) both saw significant jumps after reports from The Wall Street Journal indicated President Trump's willingness to end the military conflict with Iran. This news led to a broad market rally and a "risk-on" environment, benefiting financial firms like these due to anticipated stronger loan growth and improved credit quality in a more stable global economy. Nubank's shares, in particular, gained 3.7%, following a previous rally eight days prior spurred by similar geopolitical de-escalation news.

feed.title: BlackRock trims Lufthansa shareholding, total position lands at 4.19%
feed.summary: BlackRock, Inc. has reported a slight reduction in its total stake in Deutsche Lufthansa AG (DLAKY) to 4.19% as of March 25, 2026, down from 4.22% previously. The trimmed position includes 3.08% voting rights attached to shares and 1.10% held via instruments such as lent securities and Contracts for Difference (CFDs). This notification was made under Article 40 (1) of the WpHG, the German Securities Trading Act.

feed.title: Customer Service Orgs Set to Double Their Tech Spend, Gartner Says
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that over 50% of customer service organizations will double their technology spending by 2028, without an equivalent reduction in staff. Despite pressure to use AI to cut labor costs, organizations cannot rely solely on automation to replace human workers, as rapid headcount reduction risks operational disruption and degraded customer experience. Instead, leaders should focus on reshaping the workforce by shifting resources toward higher-value activities that support growth, as frontline roles are evolving rather than disappearing.

feed.title: Amazon ditches Amex for Mastercard
feed.summary: Amazon is ending its eight-year partnership with American Express for small business credit cards, choosing U.S. Bank and Mastercard instead. The new cards, set to launch on August 14, 2026, will offer various rewards for Amazon purchases, with no annual fees, and aim to provide small businesses with better cash flow management tools. This change specifically impacts small business credit cards and does not affect Amazon's consumer credit card offerings.

feed.title: Jury can't reach verdict in corruption trial of 2 ex-FirstEnergy executives in $60M bribery scandal
feed.summary: A jury in Ohio's corruption trial failed to reach a verdict for two former FirstEnergy Corp. executives, Chuck Jones and Michael Dowling, who were accused in a $60 million bribery scheme. The judge will consider a mistrial motion. Prosecutors alleged the executives bribed a future top utility regulator, Sam Randazzo, while the defense maintained the payment was an above-board legal settlement.

feed.title: W.R. Berkley Corp Stock: A Steady Player in Property and Casualty Insurance for North American Investors
feed.summary: W.R. Berkley Corp, a diversified insurer specializing in niche markets, offers stability for North American investors. The company's decentralized operating model and focus on complex commercial risks contribute to consistent profitability. With upcoming Q1 2026 earnings and a strong competitive position, Berkley offers a compelling investment opportunity despite cyclical industry risks.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics adds to Texas Pacific Land (NYSE: TPL) stake with buy
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a greater than ten percent owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE: TPL), reported an open-market purchase of 1 share of common stock at $494.25 per share. Following this transaction, Horizon Kinetics directly holds 3,467,936 shares of Texas Pacific Land common stock. A recent Schedule 13D amendment further indicated that Horizon Kinetics has beneficial ownership of 10,050,070 shares in total, including holdings associated with director Murray Stahl.

feed.title: TJX Companies: Fiscal 2026 Sales $60.4B, EPS $4.87 — 10‑K Summary
feed.summary: TJX Companies reported strong fiscal 2026 results with net sales of $60.376 billion, a 7% increase year over year, and diluted earnings per share of $4.87, up from $4.26 previously. The growth was driven by improved merchandise margin, continued comparable store strength across its banners, and a one-time litigation settlement benefit. The company also saw modest growth in selling square footage and plans for additional store openings in fiscal 2027.

feed.title: Scholar Rock (SRRK) Stock Surges 15.5%
feed.summary: Scholar Rock (SRRK) stock surged 15.5%, a $6.67 increase, reaching $49.75, indicating positive market response and investor confidence despite the biopharmaceutical company facing financial challenges such as zero revenue growth and negative earnings. The company, focused on rare neuromuscular diseases, has a strong liquidity position but a low Piotroski F-Score and significant insider selling, and its valuation metrics present a mixed picture with a P/B ratio near its five-year high, while analyst sentiment remains cautiously optimistic.

feed.title: ThredUp, Genesco, VF Corp, Caesars Entertainment, and Sonos Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of ThredUp, Genesco, VF Corp, Caesars Entertainment, and Sonos experienced a morning surge due to improved market sentiment. This optimism was driven by reports suggesting a potential de-escalation of the conflict in Iran and reassuring statements from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell regarding inflation and interest rates. The positive news eased concerns over geopolitical tensions and monetary policy, leading to a broad-based rally in the stock market.

feed.title: Off-price giant TJX (NYSE: TJX) runs 5,214 stores and targets 7,000
feed.summary: TJX Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TJX), a leading global off-price retailer, operates 5,214 stores worldwide across various banners like TJ Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods, and TK Maxx, and aims to expand to 7,000 locations. The company's flexible off-price model, rooted in opportunistic buying and rapid inventory turnover, allows it to offer merchandise at significant discounts. Fiscal 2026 saw a 7% increase in net sales to $60.4 billion and a diluted EPS of $4.87, despite facing risks such as intense competition, supply chain complexities, and evolving regulatory environments.

feed.title: Truist Financial Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN) Stock Price
feed.summary: Truist Financial recently lowered its price target for Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN) from $818 to $801, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a modest 4.4% upside. Despite this somewhat pessimistic revision, Regeneron generally holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, with a wide range of individual price targets. The company recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, but has also seen significant insider selling activity.

feed.title: Argus Raises Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) Price Target to $91.00
feed.summary: Argus has increased its price target for Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) to $91.00 from $80.00, reiterating a "buy" rating, which suggests a potential upside of approximately 9%. This aligns with a "Moderate Buy" consensus from other analysts, although Corteva recently reported Q4 EPS in line with expectations but missed revenue estimates, setting FY2026 guidance lower than current analyst expectations. The stock is currently trading near $83.47.

feed.title: Hershey presents snacking growth roadmap at investor day, confirms 2026 outlook
feed.summary: The Hershey Company presented its snacking growth roadmap at its investor day, reaffirming its previously announced 2026 outlook. The company detailed strategies for continued growth in the snacking sector. Further specifics on the strategic initiatives and financial projections were likely discussed during the event.

feed.title: Jury can't reach verdict in corruption trial of 2 ex-FirstEnergy executives in $60M bribery scandal
feed.summary: A jury in Ohio's corruption trial declared an impasse, unable to reach a verdict for former FirstEnergy Corp. executives Chuck Jones and Michael Dowling. They were accused of bribery, conspiracy, and aggravated theft in a $60 million scheme, including a $4.3 million payment to a future top utility regulator. The judge is now considering a mistrial motion, complicating the ongoing fallout from the large-scale corruption case that has already seen other key figures convicted.

feed.title: Ex-FirstEnergy Executives’ Corruption Trial Ends With Hung Jury
feed.summary: The corruption trial of two former FirstEnergy Corp. executives, including ex-CEO Charles Jones and former VP Michael Dowling, concluded with a hung jury after more than eight days of deliberation. This outcome leaves the possibility for the Attorney General's office to retry the executives, as neither side secured a definitive win. The trial, held in an Ohio courtroom, lasted weeks and included testimony from US Senator Jon Husted.

feed.title: ThredUp, Genesco, VF Corp, Caesars Entertainment, and Sonos Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of ThredUp, Genesco, VF Corp, Caesars Entertainment, and Sonos jumped in the morning session. This rally was driven by improved market sentiment following reports of a potential de-escalation of the conflict in Iran and reassuring comments from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell regarding inflation. The article highlights the individual stock performances and provides a brief analysis of Genesco, noting its volatility and recent dip due to consumer sentiment concerns.

feed.title: UBS Cuts PT on Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) to $56 From $59 – Here’s Why
feed.summary: UBS has lowered its price target for Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) to $56 from $59, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment is due to rising jet fuel prices, which favor higher-quality airlines with stronger margins. Similarly, Citi also cut its price target for LUV to $44 from $54, citing higher fuel costs and potential downside risks to Q1, Q2, and 2026 earnings estimates across the airline industry.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser's (WY) "Buy" Rating Reiterated at DA Davidson
feed.summary: DA Davidson has reiterated a "Buy" rating for Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY), setting a $31.00 price target that suggests a 26.5% upside. This decision comes as Weyerhaeuser reported an EPS of ($0.09), beating estimates, despite a 9.8% year-over-year revenue decline. Insider buying activity further supports investor confidence, with approximately 24,000 shares recently purchased by company insiders.

feed.title: It’s Still Card-First in the U.S.
feed.summary: New research from Global Payments Inc. indicates that credit and debit cards continue to dominate U.S. online and in-store shopping, accounting for 49% and 71% respectively in 2025. While currently card-first, digital wallets are projected to significantly increase their share in both online and in-store transactions by 2030, driven in part by Gen Z's payment preferences. Despite this shift, physical cards remain the favored funding source within digital wallets, suggesting continued relevance for card networks.

feed.title: SLB and Azule Energy Scale Enterprise Digital Operations to Strengthen Energy Delivery in Angola
feed.summary: SLB announced a three-year agreement with Azule Energy, a joint venture of bp and Eni, to expand the use of its Delfi digital platform across Azule's operations in Angola. This platform aims to enhance execution reliability, accelerate decision-making, and ensure reliable energy delivery for Azule, the largest independent energy producer in Angola. Early deployments of integrated workflows, including DrillPlan, have already demonstrated significant improvements by reducing planning cycles and increasing automation.
